title: CI gates: citation-coverage, no-advice-label, source-similarity, open-access source allowlist
context: Blocking safety/licensing CI gates for ewing-survivorship-late-effects, the top control protecting HIGH risk-tier patient-facing health content. They mechanically enforce the schema/policy spec (001): every clinical assertion sourced, the mandatory no-advice label present, the binding cancer data guardrail (open-access/aggregate/de-identified sources ONLY; controlled-access dbGaP/EGA and any identifiable patient data OUT OF SCOPE), and the fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures). These gates run before any content can be considered shippable and back the blocking dual-review gate where no patient-facing page ships without BOTH a credentialed pediatric/AYA oncologist AND a patient-advocate version-scoped sign-off (neither alone suffices).
objective: Implement the blocking safety/licensing CI gates (citation-coverage, no-advice-label, source-similarity, open-access source allowlist) with fixtures, so unsafe or improperly licensed content fails the build.
acceptanceCriteria: Citation-coverage gate: any clinical assertion without a resolvable sourceRef fails the build
No-advice-label gate: every published page must carry the 'Informational, not medical advice - consult your care team' label; a missing label fails the build
Source-similarity gate: passages too close to source text (copyright firewall) over threshold fail and route to review
Open-access source allowlist gate: a provenance record pointing at a controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA), individual-level, or identifiable source fails the build
All four gates exercised by passing and failing fixtures in CI; reviewed by the maintainer and a credentialed oncologist
output: packages/gates plus CI wiring: citation-coverage, no-advice-label, source-similarity, and open-access-allowlist checks with passing/failing fixtures
